title: Apply: food and energy in organisms
description: Apply your understanding of food and energy in organisms in this set of free practice questions aligned to NGSS standards.
topic_path: Apply: food and energy in organisms

assessment_items.question_content:
**After an animal eats food, which of these three steps occurs *first*?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Large food molecules are broken down into smaller molecules in the animal’s digestive system.
The atoms in food molecules are rearranged by chemical reactions inside cells, releasing energy.
Energy from food is used to do work in the cell.

assessment_items.question_content:
**After a cat eats food, which of these three steps occurs *second*?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Large food molecules are broken down into smaller molecules in the cat’s digestive system.
The atoms in food molecules are rearranged by chemical reactions inside cells, releasing energy.
Energy from food is used to do work in the cat’s cells.

assessment_items.question_content:
**After a dog eats food, which of these three steps occurs *last*?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Large food molecules are broken down into smaller molecules in the dog’s digestive system.
The atoms in food molecules are rearranged by chemical reactions inside cells, releasing energy.
Energy from food is used to do work in the dog’s cells.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following diagram has arrows representing the inputs and outputs of cellular respiration.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which letter best represents glucose in the model of cellular respiration?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.alt: A mitochondrion is shown in the center of the diagram. Arrows labeled A and oxygen point from the surrounding area to the mitochondrion. Arrows labeled B, C, and energy point from the mitochondrion to the surrounding area.

choices.content: A
B
C

assessment_items.question_content:
The following diagram has arrows representing the inputs and outputs of cellular respiration.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which letter best represents oxygen in the model of cellular respiration?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.alt: A mitochondrion is shown in the center of the diagram. Arrows labeled glucose and B point from the surrounding area to the mitochondrion. Arrows labeled A, C, and energy point from the mitochondrion to the surrounding area.

choices.content: A
B
C

assessment_items.question_content:
The following diagram has arrows representing the inputs and outputs of cellular respiration.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which letter best represents carbon dioxide in the model of cellular respiration?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.alt: A mitochondrion is shown in the center of the diagram. Arrows labeled A and B point from the surrounding area to the mitochondrion. Arrows labeled C, water, and energy point from the mitochondrion to the surrounding area.

choices.content: A
B
C

assessment_items.question_content:
The diagram below shows the overall process of cellular respiration. The reactants are shown to the left of the arrow, and products are shown to the right.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Based on the diagram, which statement about atoms in the reactants and products is true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A diagram shows the overall chemical equation for cellular respiration along with models of the molecules involved. According to the equation, 1 molecule of glucose (C 6 H 12 O 6) plus 6 molecules of oxygen (O 2) produce 6 molecules of carbon dioxide (C O 2) and 6 molecules of water (H 2 O). A model shows that one molecule of glucose has 6 carbon atoms, 12 hydrogen atoms, and 6 oxygen atoms. Another model shows that each oxygen molecule has 2 oxygen atoms. So, 6 molecules of oxygen have 12 oxygen atoms total. Another model shows that each carbon dioxide molecule has 2 oxygen atoms and 1 carbon atom. So, 6 molecules of carbon dioxide have 12 oxygen atoms and 6 carbon atoms total. Another model shows that each water molecule has 2 hydrogen atoms and 1 oxygen atom. So, 6 molecules of water have 12 hydrogen atoms and 6 oxygen atoms total.

choices.content: The reactants contain the same number of oxygen atoms as the products.
The reactants contain more carbon atoms than the products.
The reactants contain fewer hydrogen atoms than the products.

A model shows that one molecule of glucose has 6 carbon atoms, 12 hydrogen atoms, and 6 oxygen atoms. Another model shows that each oxygen molecule has 2 oxygen atoms. So, 6 molecules of water have 12 hydrogen atoms and 6 oxygen atoms total.

The reactants contain fewer oxygen atoms than the products.

assessment_items.question_content:
The diagram below shows the overall process of cellular respiration. The reactants are shown to the left of the arrow, and products are shown to the right.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Based on the diagram, which statement about atoms in the reactants and products is true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A diagram shows the overall chemical equation for cellular respiration along with models of the molecules involved. According to the equation, 1 molecule of glucose (C 6 H 12 O 6) plus 6 molecules of oxygen (O 2) produce 6 molecules of carbon dioxide (C O 2) and 6 molecules of water (H 2 O). A model shows that one molecule of glucose has 6 carbon atoms, 12 hydrogen atoms, and 6 oxygen atoms. Another model shows that each oxygen molecule has 2 oxygen atoms. So, 6 molecules of oxygen have 12 oxygen atoms total. Another model shows that each carbon dioxide molecule has 2 oxygen atoms and 1 carbon atom. So, 6 molecules of carbon dioxide have 12 oxygen atoms and 6 carbon atoms total. Another model shows that each water molecule has 2 hydrogen atoms and 1 oxygen atom. So, 6 molecules of water have 12 hydrogen atoms and 6 oxygen atoms total.

choices.content: The products contain the same number of oxygen atoms as the reactants.
The products contain fewer carbon atoms than the reactants.
The products contain more hydrogen atoms than the reactants.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which sentence comparing cellular respiration in plants and animals is true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Cellular respiration generates usable energy in both plant and animal cells.
Animal cells carry out cellular respiration, but plant cells do not.
Cellular respiration releases carbon dioxide in animal cells, and oxygen in plant cells.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which sentence comparing cellular respiration in plants and animals is true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Cellular respiration releases carbon dioxide in both plant and animal cells.
Animal cells carry out cellular respiration, but plant cells do not.
Cellular respiration occurs inside mitochondria in animal cells, and inside chloroplasts in plant cells.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which sentence comparing cellular respiration in plants and animals is true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Cellular respiration occurs inside the cytosol and mitochondria in both plant and animal cells.
Animal cells carry out cellular respiration, but plant cells do not.
Cellular respiration releases carbon dioxide in animal cells, and oxygen in plant cells.
